rationale: The citing paper gives De Angelis et al. (2012) a specific conceptual role in framing prior WOM-valence findings, explicitly summarizing its core result (own vs others' experiences produce opposite valence patterns via self-enhancement). It then uses that prior evidence as part of the theoretical basis for its own motive account, where self-enhancement helps explain positive sharing under low interpersonal closeness. The 2016 article does not dispute the 2012 claims; it adds a new antecedent (interpersonal closeness) and an additional motive (protecting others) to explain valence shifts.
relation_description: The citing paper extends De Angelis et al. (2012) by moving from source-of-experience differences to sender-recipient interpersonal closeness as a driver of WOM valence. It retains self-enhancement as a relevant mechanism but broadens the framework with a protect-others motive and tests consequences across transmission chains.
